specification.topic-statement: The marks system creates named conversation checkpoints and restores the conversation to any checkpoint on demand, truncating messages and rebuilding the display from the retained history.

scope.in-scope: Mark creation, the mark data record, mark storage on the agent, the rewind operation (message truncation, mark pruning, scrollback reconstruction), database persistence of mark and rewind events, and the relationship between a mark's index and the live message array.
scope.boundary: How events are rendered into scrollback lines during scrollback reconstruction is handled by the event rendering subsystem and is out of scope here.
How events are written to and read from the database is managed by the database layer and is out of scope.

field.name: message index
field.note: the number of messages in the agent's message array at the time the mark was created; this becomes the truncation point on rewind

field.name: label
field.note: optional human-readable name for the mark; null if no label was supplied

field.name: timestamp
field.note: wall-clock time when the mark was created

behavior.mark-creation:
A mark is created when the user runs the `/mark` command, optionally followed by a label string. The mark records the current size of the agent's in-memory message array as its message index and the current wall-clock time as its timestamp. The label, if provided, is stored verbatim; if omitted, the label is null.
The new mark is appended to the agent's dynamic mark array, which grows as needed. A mark creation event is persisted to the database. Persistence is best-effort: a failure is logged but does not prevent the mark from existing in memory.
behavior.rewind-operation:
Rewinding to a mark restores the conversation to the state it was in when that mark was created. The operation proceeds in four steps:
The agent's in-memory message array is truncated to the mark's message index. All messages at and beyond that index are discarded. Messages before the index are retained unchanged.
All marks whose message index is greater than the target mark's message index are removed from the agent's mark array. The target mark itself is retained, allowing the user to rewind to the same point again.
The scrollback display buffer is cleared and rebuilt by replaying all database events from the beginning of the conversation through the now- truncated message history. Each retained event is passed through the event renderer in chronological order to reconstruct the visible display.
A rewind event is persisted to the database recording that a rewind occurred. Persistence is best-effort.
The mark that was rewound to is not removed from the mark array. Subsequent rewinds to the same mark are therefore possible without recreating it.
behavior.mark-array-storage: Marks are stored in a dynamically sized pointer array on the agent. The array grows by reallocation when a new mark would exceed the current capacity. Each array slot holds a pointer to an individually allocated mark record. The array is owned by the agent and freed with it.
behavior.database-record-kinds:
Two database event kinds relate to marks:
- **mark**: written when a mark is created; stores the optional label in the data payload. - **rewind**: written when a rewind is performed; identifies the target mark in the data payload.
Both are metadata records and are not rendered to the scrollback display. During session replay, the rewind record signals the replay system to truncate the replayed event stream at the corresponding point.
behavior.message-index-semantics:
A mark's message index equals the number of messages present when the mark was created, not a zero-based index into any particular message. Rewinding to a mark truncates the array to exactly that count, preserving exactly the messages that existed before the mark was placed.
If messages are added after a mark is created and the conversation is then rewound, the mark's message index correctly identifies the truncation boundary regardless of how many messages were added in between.
